description: This bar chart compares the prediction differences (errors) between two machine learning models—standard Extra Trees and Genetic Algorithm-optimized Extra Trees (GA-Extra Trees)—for yield strength prediction in medium-manganese low-density steels. The x-axis displays three annealing temperatures (750°C, 800°C, and 850°C) corresponding to experimental samples ANT750, ANT800, and ANT850 of the Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si alloy system processed via vacuum induction melting, hot rolling, cold rolling, and annealing. The y-axis quantifies the model prediction error in MPa. At 750°C, Extra Trees exhibits a 34 MPa difference versus 12 MPa for GA-Extra Trees; at 800°C, 31 MPa versus 10 MPa; and at 850°C, 36 MPa versus 19 MPa. The data reveals that the GA-optimized model consistently achieves substantially lower prediction errors across all processing conditions, reducing discrepancies by approximately 65% on average compared to the baseline model. This figure supports the conclusion that hyperparameter optimization via Genetic Algorithm significantly enhances the predictive accuracy of Extra Trees regression for yield strength in multi-phase steels.
